source_alignment.subject: Mathematics
source_alignment.source_statement: Solve problems involving the relative sizes of two quantities where missing values can be found by using integer multiplication and division facts.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack focuses on preserving multiplicative relationships in scale and ratio tables.

objective.child_goal: I can scale quantities by keeping the same relationship.
objective.statement: Use multiplicative scaling to solve ratio and proportion problems.
misconceptions: Uses additive change instead of multiplicative scaling.
Scales one quantity but forgets to scale the matching quantity.
Reverses the ratio order and compares the wrong quantities.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The number you multiply by to make a matching larger or smaller amount.
vocabulary.audio_script: A scale factor tells how many times bigger or smaller the matching amount is.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A slider multiplying both sides of a ratio table.

vocabulary.audio_script: A ratio compares one amount with another amount.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Two linked bars labelled in matching order.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: If one quest badge needs 3 gems, how many gems do 4 badges need?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Activate multiplicative equal-group thinking before ratio tables.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: One badge has three gems; four badges copy the group four times.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: The academy recipe only works if both quantities scale together.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce ratio as a relationship that must be preserved.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: If one side is multiplied, the matching side must be multiplied too.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A ratio table locks two quantities together with a scale rail.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: 2 blue crystals match 5 red crystals. For 6 blue crystals, multiply both parts by 3.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model finding a scale factor and applying it to both parts.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Two becomes six by multiplying by three, so five also multiplies by three.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A table row scales from 2:5 to 6:15.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Drag the scale factor onto both matching quantities.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Make the same multiplicative operation visible on both sides.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The same scale factor travels to both quantities.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A multiplier token snaps onto both columns.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Did this learner add 4 or multiply by 3?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Detect additive reasoning in a proportional situation.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Check whether the relationship stayed the same.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two solution paths compare adding and multiplying.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach the coach why both quantities must scale together.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Use explanation to confirm multiplicative relationship.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Can you show why the same multiplier belongs on both sides?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The coach scales one column only and waits for correction.

manipulatives.purpose: Represent proportional relationships in linked rows so both quantities scale together.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners complete ratio tables, drag scale factors and compare additive and multiplicative paths.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Keyboard controls move between table cells; reduced-motion mode uses static multiplier labels.

manipulatives.purpose: Develop flexible proportional reasoning by constructing and comparing unit-rate, integer-factor and partitioning routes to the same scaled quantity.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners build a scale route, keep both quantities linked, test whether an additive shortcut preserves the ratio and defend the most efficient valid strategy for the numbers given.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Scale steps use text multipliers, spoken equations and shape-linked quantities; keyboard controls, direct factor entry and single-switch scanning avoid drag dependence, while static and reduced-motion modes show an ordered calculation ladder with instant updates and no stretching animation.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave recipes, maps and similar-shape contexts only after table scaling is secure.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Complete ratio tables using integer scale factors.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: base ratio + target known value + missing paired value
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Check scale factors are accessible from known multiplication facts before stretch mode.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Make the same scale factor apply visibly to both related quantities.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: two linked quantities + multiplier token + table completion
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Ensure visual layout keeps ratio order clear.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Use distractors that reveal additive reasoning in proportional problems.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: ratio problem + correct scaled answer + additive distractors
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Every additive distractor should route to the additive-trap repair.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Transfer scale reasoning to maps, recipes and simple similar-shape contexts.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: context + paired quantity table + missing value
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep contexts concise and avoid hidden unit conversion.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Feed spaced review with prior weak ratio relationships and scale factors.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review scheduler selects ratio family and misconception route
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use learner error history to select additive or order-reversal probes.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Interpret ratio order, part-to-part and part-to-whole language across concrete and visual representations.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: labelled groups + ratio statement + representation or language choice
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep labels persistent and do not rely on colour alone.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Find one-unit values and rescale proportional relationships efficiently.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: known pair + one-unit row + target row + unit-aware explanation
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use whole-number unit rates in core items and show both divide and multiply stages.

body.prompt: 2 blue crystals need 5 red crystals. How many red crystals match 6 blue crystals?

hints: 2 blue becomes 6 blue by multiplying by 3.
Multiply the red crystals by the same scale factor.
question_variants.explanation: 2 is scaled to 6 by multiplying by 3, so 5 must also multiply by 3 to make 15.

body.prompt: A map scale says 3 cm represents 12 km. What does 6 cm represent?

hints: 3 cm becomes 6 cm by multiplying by 2.
Multiply 12 km by the same scale factor.
question_variants.explanation: The scale factor is 2, so 12 km becomes 24 km.

body.prompt: Scale the ratio 4:7 by 3.

hints: Multiply both parts by 3.
4 x 3 is 12 and 7 x 3 is 21.
question_variants.explanation: Scaling 4:7 by 3 gives 12:21 because both parts are multiplied by 3.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Uses additive change instead of multiplicative scaling.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner chooses an answer made by adding the same difference instead of multiplying.
repair_sequence: Show the multiplication relationship between known values.
Compare additive and multiplicative paths side by side.
Ask which path keeps the ratio the same.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner rejects an additive distractor in a new proportional problem.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Scales one quantity but forgets to scale the matching quantity.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner changes one column while leaving the paired value unchanged.
repair_sequence: Lock both quantities with a relationship rail.
Move the multiplier token to both sides.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner applies the same scale factor to both quantities.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Reverses the ratio order and compares the wrong quantities.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner swaps the first and second quantities in a ratio table.
repair_sequence: Colour-code each quantity and keep labels visible.
Trace the first quantity down the table.
Rebuild the ratio in the original order.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner preserves ratio order in a new table.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use calm table highlights and no flashing wrong states.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace multiplier travel with static labels.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read ratio language aloud and pause between quantities.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Keep contexts concise and show labelled table columns.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Reveal one row and one scale decision at a time.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow calculator-free working space and no timers.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Frame additive errors as common traps to test.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach ratio, scale, times as many and matching quantity.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires using integer scale factors in ratio tables; secure requires avoiding additive traps and transferring the relationship to simple contexts.
evidence.parent_summary: Can scale matching quantities together to solve ratio and proportion problems.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Identifies scale factors, applies them to both quantities and explains why additive change does not preserve the ratio.
next_actions: Add missing-value problems with larger scale factors.
Interleave maps, recipes and similar-shape contexts.
Move to non-integer scale factors only after integer scaling is secure.

qa.notes: Depth-wave review bank reaches the 240-item pilot target with three preserved curated questions and 237 deterministic candidates covering ratio language, equivalent ratios, scaling, recipes and maps, unitising, proportion, missing values, representations and misconception repair. Generated candidates include concrete-to-visual ratio scaffolds, keyboard/switch/oral and non-drag interactions, rich scale-factor/unit/check feedback and strategic mission progress earned through reasoning rather than speed. Human curriculum, teacher, SEND, accessibility, safeguarding and renderer review remains required before promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
